about.description: I am a joint J.D./M.B.A. student and founder of Sonomos. My work sits at the intersection of law, artificial intelligence, blockchain, and regulatory systems. I approach legal problems by focusing on how emerging technologies fundamentally challenge existing frameworks and workflows. Further, my research covers areas such as agentic AI, market manipulation, crypto regulation, blockchain interoperability, privacy, and compliance. At Sonomos, we are building local-first, privacy infrastructure designed to protect sensitive information from AI systems across all surfaces. I am also a member of LegalQuants, an exclusive, international community of legal professionals focused on building towards the quantitative and technological transformation, and subsequent implementation, of the modern legal practice. Before graduate school, I worked in government and policy, including roles with U.S. Senator Alex Padilla and Congressman Scott Peters. My experience spans legal research, regulatory analysis, business strategy, and emerging technology. More broadly, I focus on translating complex technical and legal challenges into clear analysis, persuasive writing, and practical systems.

basics.description: I’m Zachary Brenner (or Zack), an AI-native JD/MBA student, LegalQuant, and founder. I build and write at the intersection of AI, blockchain, privacy, compliance, and digital markets, focusing on how legal and regulatory systems must evolve to meet emerging technologies.
basics.summary: AI-native JD/MBA building at the intersection of law, blockchain, and emerging technology
basics.keywords: Zachary Brenner, Zack Brenner, LegalQuant, AI law, blockchain law, privacy, compliance, crypto regulation

projects.name: DanceCard (Upcoming)
projects.description: An interactive event discovery platform designed specifically for seniors, aiming to reduce loneliness and social isolation by making it extremely simple to find and attend social events. The product prioritizes usability and accessibility, removing the friction and intimidation often associated with platforms like Eventbrite.

highlights: Building a privacy and AI compliance layer designed to detect and protect sensitive information before it is exposed to external AI systems. Sonomos operates as a local-first infrastructure that sits between users and AI tools, identifying and mitigating risks such as confidential data leakage, regulatory exposure, and improper data handling. The system emphasizes real-time detection/protection and compliance visibility for professionals and organizations adopting AI.
Supporting legal and business operations with research, drafting, and analysis in a global executive coaching and advisory organization.
Worked on regulatory strategy and business development initiatives within the cryptocurrency and compliance space.
Worked in the civil division supporting judicial chambers with legal research and analysis.
